mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments that require efficient orchestration of large language models while managing high-scale traffic. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for appropriate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the llm-d platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64

chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64

sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> Download the CLI for Linux
> Make the CLI executable
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud, specifying the desired version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --num-nodes=3 --cluster-version=1.31 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to verify their status

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository
> Install llm-d in the llm-d namespace

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration
> Apply monitoring configuration

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> Uninstall the llm-d Helm release
> Delete the llm-d namespace and all resources within it

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, performing the upgrade, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d

```

> Drain all pods in the llm-d namespace
> Upgrade llm-d to the latest version
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade
commands: kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a pod is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace llm-d' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale your cluster by adding more nodes or increasing the size of existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the specified container image does not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d' showing all pods in the Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
